person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Disputed Identity«/b»
Mary Doggett was the wife of George Lewis, who emigrated from England to Scituate, Massachusetts, in 1634. Traditionally his wife has been identified as Sarah Jenkins, but researchers have determined that this identification is incorrect, and that Mary Doggett was his wife and the mother of at least his first four children.

«b»Biography«/b»
Mary Doggett was a daughter of Thomas Doggett and his wife Isabell, baptized at Horsmonden, Kent, England, on 21 September 1600. 

Mary was married to George Lewis on 1 November 1624 at Horsmonden, Kent, England.

George Lewis migrated in 1634. However, Remington places Mary's arrival between 20 Sep 1635, when her husband was admitted to the church but she was not, and June 1637, when her child was born in Massachusetts.

«b»The Legal Skirmish in Kent«/b»
Among those who wanted to take a chance in the New World was George Lewis, a clothier in Brenchley, Kent. There was only one problem, George and his wife, Mary Doggett, had four children below the age of 9. While mulling his choices, George and the family moved to Staplehurst, Kent.

When the opportunity came, George took it. In 1634, he and his brother John sailed from England to Scituate, Plymouth Colony, leaving behind Mary, with four children and heavily pregnant with a fifth.

By the 16th century, England, like most European countries, had a social safety net of sorts. (It wasn't until the 19th century that the grim workhouses described by Dickens were instituted.) After all, "charity" is one of the seven Christian virtues, and most people of the time considered the existence of poverty as an inevitability, rather than as a character flaw of the impoverished. In England, support for the poor was accomplished via a Poor Tax levied on residents and businesses by the local church. The parish administered the use of these funds to support those in need, usually persons who were old, infirm, orphaned, or who otherwise lacked a suitable worker in the family.

So, the solution for Mary Lewis and her family of four-going-on-five was obvious: go on welfare.

The parishioners of Staplehurst had a different opinion. Such a large family would be a big drain on the parish funds. Maybe they didn't want to deny assistance to Mary, but they thought someone else should pay for it. After all, the first four children had been baptized in Brenchley and George had been born in Brenchley.

The 1598 Vagrancy and Poor Relief Acts said that a "wife and children under seven years of age should go to the place where the husband belongs" and that "children over seven should go to the place of their birth." So, the Staplehurst parish attempted to have her sent back to Brenchley.

The Brenchley parish disagreed, and the matter ended up in court.

At its Easter Sessions, on 15 April 1634, the court considered the case. Applying the law, the oldest child, who was older than seven, could have been sent back to Brenchley, but the wife and the younger children could be sent to the place where the "husband belonged". The court decided that the husband had become a legitimate resident of Staplehurst and elected to ignore the age of the oldest child: "It is ordered by this court that the said Mary Lewis and her children be settled and remain in Staplehurst where they now are, and the said parishioners of Staplehurst are to see them provided for according to the law."

The fifth child was born a month later and baptized in Staplehurst. It's unknown whether Mary's family was treated well while living in Staplehurst. Within three years, Mary and the children emigrated to New England, where she and George had three more children. Despite the hardships, all eight survived to adulthood. 

George Lewis (clothier) was one of those dismissed from Plymouth Church in 1634, 'in case they join in a body at Scituate.' He had lands here [Scituate] in 1633. His house lot was on Kent street, the first south of Meeting-house lane. He came from East Greenwich in Kent. 

She died after July 3, 1676, in Barnstable, Massachusetts. 

«b»Children include:«/b»
1.) Mary Lewis (ca 1625-2 Jul 1655)

2.) George Lewis (ca 1627-)

3.) Thomas Lewis (ca 1630-May 1709)

4.) Lieut. James Lewis (ca 1632-4 Oct 1713)

5.) Edward Lewis (ca 1634-29 Mar 1703)

6.) John Lewis (Died unmarried) (2 Mar 1637/8-26 Mar 1676)

7.) Ephraim Lewis (23 Jul 1641-aft 3 Mar 1663)

8.) Sarah Lewis (2 Feb 1643/4-11 Feb 1734/5

zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Doggett於1600年9月21日出生在英國英格蘭肯特霍斯蒙登時，她的父親Thomas Doggett28歲，母親Isabell Martin25歲。她於1624年11月1日與George Lewis Jr.在英國肯特霍斯蒙登結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1676年7月3日之後在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Doggett于1600年9月21日出生在英国英格兰肯特霍斯蒙登时，她的父亲Thomas Doggett28岁，母亲Isabell Martin25岁。她于1624年11月1日与George Lewis Jr.在英国肯特霍斯蒙登结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1676年7月3日之后在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波。

nameMeanings.name: Doggett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Duckett with voicing of the medial consonant. Alternatively, the name may also be from a Middle English rhyming pet form of Roger (see Dodge ) + the diminutive suffix -et. But whether the medial -g- was pronounced as a stop /g/ (as in dog) or an affricate /dʒ/ (as in Roger) is impossible to tell.

variants.name: Duckett
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Duket. This may sometimes have been an early Middle English pet form of an Old English Ducc(a), but was more often a pet form of Marmaduke, especially in Yorkshire (see Duke ). Marmaduke itself is from Irish Maol Maedóc ‘servant of Maedóc’, Maedóc being the name of prominent Irish saints of the 6th and 7th centuries.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Doggett was born on 21 September 1600, in Horsmonden, Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Doggett, was 28 and her mother, Isabell Martin, was 25. She married George Lewis Jr. on 1 November 1624, in Horsmonden, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died after 3 July 1676, in Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, and was buried in Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Doggett est née le 21 septembre 1600, à Horsmonden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Doggett, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Isabell Martin, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé George Lewis Jr. le 1 novembre 1624, à Horsmonden, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée après 3 juillet 1676, à Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, et a été enterrée à Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

November 1624 in Horsmonden, Kent, England George Lewis Jr. geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb nach 3. it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Doggett è nata il il 21 settembre 1600 nel Horsmonden, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Thomas Doggett, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Isabell Martin, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato George Lewis Jr. il 1 novembre 1624, nel Horsmonden, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì dopo il 3 luglio 1676, nel Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, e fu sepolta a Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Doggettが1600年9月21日にイギリスイギリスケントホールスモンデンで生まれた時，父Thomas Doggettは28歳，母Isabell Martinは25歳であった。1624年11月1日にイギリスケントホールスモンデンでGeorge Lewis Jr.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1676年7月3日以後にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーバーンスタブルで死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーバーンスタブルにて埋葬された。

ko.Rehoboth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay, Br: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 브리스톨 레홉스
ko.Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Br: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일
ko.Scituate, Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay, B: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 플림우스 스시투앳
ko.Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Col: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 바른스타블일
ko.Scituate, Plymouth Colony, British Colon: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 스시투앳
ko.lifeSummary: Mary Doggett(이)가 1600년 9월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 호르스몽에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Doggett(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabell Martin(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1624년 11월 1일 영국 켄트 호르스몽에서 George Lewis Jr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1676년 7월 3일 이후 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 바른스타블일에서 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 바른스타블일에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Doggett nasceu em 21 de setembro de 1600, em Horsmonden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas Doggett, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Isabell Martin, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com George Lewis Jr. em 1 de novembro de 1624, em Horsmonden, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu após 3 de julho de 1676, em Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, e foi sepultada em Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Doggett родилась 21 сентября 1600 г. в местности хаурсмонден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Doggett было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabell Martin, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George Lewis Jr. 1 ноября 1624 г. в местности хаурсмонден, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла после 3 июля 1676 г. в местности барнстабл, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика и была похоронена в местности барнстабл, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Doggett nació el 21 de septiembre de 1600, en Horsmonden, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas Doggett, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Isabell Martin, tenía 25 años. Se casó con George Lewis Jr. el 1 de noviembre de 1624, en Horsmonden, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió después de 3 de julio de 1676, en Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, y fue sepultada en Barnstable, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
